mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in PAYEMS signals expanding labor income and lower near-term recession risk, raising expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 142654.0 thousand as of the latest available month before 2015-11-15
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with continuing payroll growth
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS growth is followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor demand and rising recession probability, causing equity investors to cut earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 5.0 percent as of the latest available month before 2015-11-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion or early labor-market deterioration
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: An upward crossing in FEDFUNDS from the zero-rate region signals tighter discount-rate conditions, lowering equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.25 percentage points over 3 months after being below 0.25 percent
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 0.12 percent as of the latest available month before 2015-11-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening liftoff from the zero lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS increases are followed by nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if downside hit rate after triggers is below 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise over 3 months, household spending is supported by labor income rather than credit alone, increasing expected sales growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 447255.0 million dollars as of the latest available month before 2015-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if observations with simultaneous positive 3-month RSAFS and PAYEMS growth do not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than observations with only one series rising, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO below its recent trend signals weakening factory output and operating leverage, leading investors to reduce cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average and its 3-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 107.0917 as of the latest available month before 2015-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO slowdowns are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if triggered returns are not lower than unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST signals stronger construction activity and future demand for housing-linked goods, improving cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is above its 12-month average and its 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1206.0 thousand units as of the latest available month before 2015-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST uptrend triggers fail to deliver positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if returns following triggers are not higher than returns following non-trigger periods.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25 percent, real policy accommodation increases, supporting risk appetite and equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent and CPIAUCSL has a positive 6-month change
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 237.566 and FEDFUNDS was 0.12 percent in the latest available snapshot before 2015-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS below 0.25 percent produces lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than either condition alone or if the interaction term is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in GDPC1 growth below trend signals broad demand weakness and future earnings downgrades, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1.0 percent
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 16394.2 billions of chained 2017 dollars in the latest available quarterly snapshot before 2015-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth below 1.0 percent is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or if the average triggered return is not below zero.
